text: Chronic low back pain for ~6 years, dull ache across the beltline, currently 3/10, worse with prolonged sitting and carrying laundry up stairs
transcript_quote: Same one, going on six years now. A dull ache right across the beltline. Worse after I sit at the computer doing applications all day, or when I haul the laundry up to the third floor. Today it is about a three out of ten.

text: No numbness, tingling, or weakness in legs; no bladder or bowel dysfunction
transcript_quote: No, none of that. It stays in the back.

text: Back pain relieved by stretching and heat
transcript_quote: Stretching helps some. Heat helps more.

text: Patient stopped refilling all prior medications months ago after insurance switched to wife's plan
transcript_quote: I ran out of everything months ago and stopped refilling when the insurance switched over to my wife's plan.

text: Previously diagnosed hypertension was never treated; patient did not start prescribed pills due to cost
transcript_quote: the blood pressure problem we diagnosed a while back never actually got treated. You were meant to start pills and it fell through.

text: Office blood pressure 106/67 mmHg
transcript_quote: Your pressure here in the office is actually fine — one-oh-six over sixty-seven

text: Elevated home blood pressure readings ~150s systolic most mornings
transcript_quote: My wife's machine says one-fifty-something most mornings.

text: Started lisinopril 10 mg oral tablet once daily in the morning for hypertension
transcript_quote: lisinopril ten milligrams once a day

text: Started amlodipine 2.5 mg oral tablet once daily in the morning for hypertension
transcript_quote: amlodipine two and a half milligrams once a day

text: Started hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet once daily in the morning for hypertension
transcript_quote: hydrochlorothiazide twenty-five milligrams in the morning — that last one is a water pill

text: Counseled to call if dizziness, lightheadedness, dry cough, or ankle swelling occurs with new BP medications

slots.topic: medication side effects to watch for

transcript_quote: If you get dizzy, lightheaded, a nagging dry cough, or ankle swelling, call me

text: Started scheduled acetaminophen (Tylenol) 325 mg oral tablet every 4-6 hours for back pain
transcript_quote: I want you on scheduled acetaminophen — plain Tylenol, the three-hundred-twenty-five-milligram tablets I am sending to the pharmacy

text: Counseled on movement for back pain: walking, stretches, avoid prolonged sitting, stand every 30 minutes with a timer
transcript_quote: the real medicine is movement: walking, the stretches we went over years ago, and no marathon sitting. Set a timer, stand up every half hour

text: Unemployed since April after warehouse shift was cut; financial strain

slots.detail: job loss April, seeking work, money tight

transcript_quote: The warehouse cut my whole shift in April, so I am out of work. I have been applying everywhere, nothing yet.

text: Poor housing conditions: broken A/C for 3 weeks (95°F indoors), leak/mold under kitchen sink, broken elevator requiring stair climbs; unresponsive landlord
transcript_quote: The air conditioning has been dead three weeks — it hit ninety-five in there last weekend. There is a leak under the kitchen sink growing something I do not want to name, and the elevator has been broken since we moved in

text: Provided tenant rights packet and county housing hotline information from front office
transcript_quote: Our front office keeps a packet on tenant rights and the county housing hotline. I am having them print it for you before you leave.

text: Reports job-loss stress with occasional early morning waking (~4 a.m.) but coping; not falling apart
transcript_quote: I am stressed, I will not lie. I wake up at four some mornings doing math in my head. But I am not falling apart.

text: GAD-7 score of 1, minimal anxiety; assessed as situational stress, not an anxiety disorder
transcript_quote: you scored a one, which is minimal, and that matches how you describe it. Stress, not an anxiety disorder.

text: Counseled to report early if stress worsens to nightly episodes or mood declines
transcript_quote: If the four a.m. math sessions become every night, or the mood starts sinking, I want to hear about it early, not late.

text: Minimal alcohol use (a beer on Sundays); AUDIT-C score of 1, no concern
transcript_quote: an AUDIT-C of one, no concern at all

text: Former smoker, quit almost 20 years ago
transcript_quote: Almost twenty years quit. Teenage me smoked; grown me cannot afford it anyway.

text: Gingivitis: lower gum line red, swollen, bleeds with light touch and on brushing

slots.findings: red swollen gums, bleeding on light touch

transcript_quote: The gum line along the bottom teeth is red and swollen, and it bleeds with just a light touch. That is gingivitis.

text: Referral placed to dental clinic (sliding-scale) for cleaning and gingivitis care
transcript_quote: I am putting in a referral to the dental clinic; they run a sliding-scale fee

text: Administered influenza vaccine (trivalent, split virus, PF) in left arm
transcript_quote: flu shot. You are due, and it costs you nothing with the visit.

text: Follow-up in 4-6 weeks with home blood pressure log to assess new medications
transcript_quote: I want you back in four to six weeks with the home blood pressure log so we can watch the new pills work.

text: BMI 29.31 kg/m2 (height 162.1 cm, weight 77 kg)
Heart rate 86 /min, respiratory rate 14 /min
PRAPARE assessment: worried about losing housing, unable to obtain utilities in past year; feels safe at home, strong social contact; private insurance; household of 4

slots.findings: housing insecurity worry, utilities unmet need

text: Assessment of health and social care needs completed

slots.procedure: health and social care needs assessment
